data.title: Trump’s crypto czar meets UAE’s national security adviser on crypto, AI
data.description:
White House crypto and AI czar David Sacks was among a number of Trump administration officials who recently met with United Arab Emirates officials to discuss emerging technologies and UAE’s potential plans to increase its investment in the United States. 
Sheikh Tahnoon Bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the Gulf nation’s national security adviser and brother of the country’s president, said in a March 20 X post that he had spoken with Sacks about AI’s impact, “the expanding role of digital currencies” along with “the investment opportunities emerging at their convergence.”
US President Donald Trump hosted Tahnoon for dinner at the White House on March 18 and posted on his Truth Social platform a day later that he and senior US officials discussed with Tahnoon “ways for our countries to increase our partnership for the advancing of our economic and technological futures.”


Tahnoon (left) meeting with Trump (right) in the Oval Office
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/trump-crypto-czar-sacks-talk-uae-senior-official-on-crypto-ai?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

source: https://coinpedia.org/crypto-live-news/sec-confirms-bitcoin-pow-mining-not-securities/

data.title: Australia outlines crypto regulation plan, promises action on debanking
data.description:
Australia’s government, under its ruling center-left Labor Party, has proposed a new crypto framework regulating exchanges under existing financial services laws and has promised to tackle debanking.
It comes ahead of a federal election slated to be held on or before May 17, which current polling shows is shaping up to a dead heat between Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s Labor and the opposing Coalition led by Peter Dutton.
The Treasury Department said in a March 21 statement that crypto exchanges, custody services and some brokerage firms that trade or store crypto will come under the new laws.
The regime imposes similar compliance requirements as other financial services in the country, such as following rules safeguarding customer assets, obtaining an Australian Financial Services Licence and meeting minimum capital requirements
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/australian-treasury-outlines-crypto-regulation-to-tackle-debanking?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: ETH mega pump coming? Ether on exchanges falls to near-decade low
data.description:
Ether’s supply on crypto exchanges has dropped to its lowest level since November 2015, leading some analysts to predict a major price rally despite recent bearish sentiment.
“Ethereum's holders have now brought the available supply on exchanges down to 8.97M, the lowest amount in nearly 10 years (November, 2015),” crypto analytics platform Santiment said in a March 20 X post.


Ether’s supply on crypto exchanges has reached its lowest point since November 2015
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ethereum-supply-decline-crypto-exchanges-price-prediction-analysts?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.description:

data.title: Binance will launch the first batch of delisting votes on March 21
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance announced that it will launch the "Vote to Delist" mechanism from March 21, 2025. The community can vote to delist projects with monitoring labels. The voting period is 7 days, and the deadline is 23:59 (UTC) on March 27, 2025. Users must hold at least 0.01 BNB and log in to a verified account to participate in the voting. The voting results will be included in Binance's official review, but will not be used as the basis for the final decision.

data.title: South Korea Targets Unregistered Crypto Exchanges
data.description:
South Korea's FIU takes action against unregistered overseas cryptocurrency exchanges like BitMEX and KuCoin, with potential website blocks for exchanges serving Korean users.
source: https://coincu.com/327733-south-korea-crypto-exchange-action

data.title: BlackRock thinks Bitcoin could thrive in a recession – Will it?
data.description:
Market sentiment on Bitcoin's ability to hedge against economic pressure remains mixed.
source: https://ambcrypto.com/blackrock-thinks-bitcoin-could-thrive-in-a-recession-will-it/

data.title: South Korea Takes Action Against Unregistered Crypto Exchanges
data.description: According to Odaily, the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) under South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) has initiated enforcement actions against overseas cryptocurrency exchanges that provide services to South Korean users without registration. This move is in accordance with the Act on Reporting and Using Specified Financial Transaction Information. Reports indicate that the FIU is considering blocking website access as a primary enforcement measure.

data.title: Ave.ai has fully supported PumpSwap transactions on the product side
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Ave.ai has now fully supported PumpSwap transactions on the product side, including Ave App, AveSniperBot and PC, and supports real-time synchronization of PumpSwap market K-line and full data insights.
